question.text: Securities Transaction Tax (STT) on mutual fund transactions is applicable in which of the following scenarios?

answer.text: On switch transactions between debt schemes
On purchase of equity mutual fund units
On sale (redemption) of equity mutual fund units
On purchase and sale of debt mutual fund units

explanation.text: **The Logic:** STT is applicable only on the SALE (redemption/switch-out) of equity-oriented mutual fund units — not on purchase, and not on debt/liquid/money market fund transactions. The rate is 0.001% for delivery-based and 0.025% for non-delivery transactions. **The Trap:** In direct equity markets, STT applies on both buy and sell sides. Candidates often extend this to mutual funds, but MF STT is only on the sell side for equity-oriented schemes. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers STT rates; Series VIII covers STT in the derivatives context where rates differ significantly. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** STT paid on equity MF redemption allows the investor to claim LTCG at a concessional rate of 12.5% (for gains above Rs.1.25 lakh). Always verify STT was charged — it appears in the redemption statement and is relevant for tax filing.
